source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly with each reinforcement layer as a distinct body. Multipart decomposition exposes the soil lifts, paper reinforcement strips, plywood face panels, and base course as distinct part families.

full_prompt:
Design the ASCE CN5 Geo-Challenge 1.0 m wide x 0.70 m tall mechanically stabilized earth (MSE) retaining wall as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the wall as the physical collection of stacked soil lifts, horizontal paper reinforcement strips, plywood face panels, and a base course. The original prompt already specifies 'deliver STEP AP242 assembly of the wall with each reinforcement layer as a distinct body'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition with bonded + pullout interfaces for the reinforcement-to-soil load path.

The assembly consists of four part families: (A) horizontal soil lifts, each 100 mm thick and 1.0 m wide spanning the full retained-fill depth (approximately 0.60 m behind the face) and the wall footprint, built up in 7 lifts to the 0.70 m wall height; (B) paper reinforcement strips, 60 gsm kraft paper with 8 N per 25 mm tensile strength (320 N per meter width), placed between each pair of lifts on a schedule the designer selects (typical spacing every 100-200 mm vertical, strip length selected to satisfy pullout and active-wedge length); (C) plywood face panels on the vertical face of the wall (1.0 m wide per panel, approximately 100-200 mm tall per panel, stacked 4-7 panels high to the 0.70 m wall face height); (D) the base course / foundation (compacted sand or rigid foundation platform) that provides the frictional base for sliding resistance.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: face width 1.0 m, face height 0.70 m, lift thickness 100 mm, vertical face, backfill region 0.60 m deep behind face.

Material inherits verbatim: backfill sand with unit weight 17.0 kN/m^3 and friction angle phi 32 deg; paper reinforcement 60 gsm with tensile strength 320 N/m width; plywood face non-load-bearing.

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 retained soil Coulomb active earth pressure distribution; LC2 surcharge 4.8 kPa over 0.30 m wide patch 50 mm behind face; LC3 pluck-equivalent static load of 1.1 kN (from 45 kg dropped 0.30 m) on top of surcharge patch.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) sliding factor of safety >= 1.5 against horizontal thrust from retained soil + surcharge; (R2) overturning factor of safety about the toe >= 2.0; (R3) internal pullout factor of safety of the critical reinforcement layer >= 1.5 using Coulomb active earth pressure + pullout resistance; (R4) reinforcement tensile rupture factor of safety >= 1.5 against paper ultimate tensile strength; (R5) total paper reinforcement mass <= 20 g. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: paper-soil pullout DCR and paper rupture DCR at each reinforcement layer must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. A classical Coulomb/Rankine earth-pressure limit-equilibrium calculator is required outside the FEA for sliding, overturning, pullout, and rupture factor-of-safety resolution. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (each soil lift, each reinforcement strip, each face panel, the base course) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..D) and instance_index, plus a geotech report listing sliding, overturning, pullout, and rupture FoS, total paper mass, assembly mass, and the pullout / rupture DCR per reinforcement layer.
short_prompt:
Design an ASCE CN5 Geo-Challenge mechanically stabilized earth (MSE) retaining wall as a multi-part assembly (stacked 100 mm soil lifts, paper reinforcement strips, plywood face panels, base course).

Face 1.0 m wide x 0.70 m tall with 7 x 100 mm lifts and backfill 0.60 m deep. Backfill unit weight 17.0 kN/m3, friction angle phi 32 deg; paper reinforcement 60 gsm with tensile strength 320 N/m; plywood face non-load-bearing.

Load cases: LC1 retained-soil Coulomb active earth pressure; LC2 surcharge 4.8 kPa over 0.30 m patch 50 mm behind face; LC3 pluck-equivalent 1100 N from 45 kg dropped 0.30 m.

Pass/fail: sliding FoS >= 1.5; overturning FoS >= 2.0; pullout FoS >= 1.5; rupture FoS >= 1.5; total paper mass <= 20 g; FEM at assembled level; per-layer pullout/rupture DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Active/passive Coulomb wedges, sliding, overturning, and pullout are closed-form limit-equilibrium calculations, not FEA.

prompt.title: 0.7 m MSE wall with paper reinforcement (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part paper-reinforced MSE wall: stacked 100 mm soil lifts (A) + horizontal paper reinforcement strips (B) + plywood face panels (C) + base course (D). 1.0 m wide x 0.70 m tall face. Verified at assembly level against sliding FoS >= 1.5, overturning FoS >= 2.0, pullout FoS >= 1.5, rupture FoS >= 1.5, paper mass <= 20 g, and per-layer pullout/rupture DCR <= 1.0.

parts.name: soil_lift
parts.role: horizontal lift of compacted backfill sand 100 mm thick x 1.0 m wide x ~0.60 m deep; provides self-weight confining stress for reinforcement pullout and the mass of the wall for sliding + overturning resistance
parts.count_note: 7 lifts stacked at 100 mm to reach the 0.70 m face height
parts.material_hint: sand, unit weight 17.0 kN/m^3, phi 32 deg

parts.name: paper_reinforcement_strip
parts.role: horizontal 60 gsm kraft-paper reinforcement strip placed between lifts; carries active-wedge thrust through tensile membrane action and pullout friction against the surrounding soil
parts.count_note: Typically one strip between each pair of 7 lifts (6 interfaces); strip length team-selected to satisfy pullout + active-wedge length

parts.name: plywood_face_panel
parts.role: plywood face panel stacked vertically on the front of the wall, 1.0 m wide per panel; non-load-bearing facing that confines the soil locally and transmits the face pressure distribution into the pinned connection between lifts
parts.count_note: 4-7 stacked panels to cover the 0.70 m face height

parts.name: base_course
parts.role: compacted sand or rigid foundation platform beneath the wall footprint that provides the frictional base for sliding resistance (coefficient tan(32 deg))

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 1.0 m wide x 0.60 m deep x 0.70 m tall
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: wall coordinates: +x along 1.0 m face width, +y into retained fill, +z up

load_cases.name: pluck_equivalent
load_cases.derivation: 45 kg dropped 0.30 m reduced to equivalent static
load_cases.application: vertical on top of surcharge patch

boundary_conditions.base: frictional base with coefficient tan(32 deg)
boundary_conditions.reinforcement_to_soil: pullout friction developed over the full strip length behind the Coulomb active wedge

objective.primary: pass all FoS under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: each paper reinforcement layer must satisfy pullout and rupture demand-to-capacity under the combined LC1+LC2+LC3 envelope
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: 2D plane-strain continuum or limit-equilibrium hand calc; reinforcement as truss/membrane with tied-pullout interface to soil lifts

interfaces.load_path: reinforcement-to-soil pullout friction over the strip length behind the active Coulomb wedge; develops reinforcement tensile membrane action
soil-to-face pinned interaction: the lifts confine the plywood face panels laterally and the face panels redistribute local lift pressures
frictional base interface between the lowest lift and the base course; provides sliding resistance equal to the total wall weight times tan(phi)
optional anchorage of the reinforcement strip into the plywood face (team-selected detail); transfers reinforcement tension into the face panel

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original sample already calls out body_count=assembly with each reinforcement layer as a distinct body; the multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition and adds the soil-lift and face-panel part families.
notes.speculation_caveats: Lift count (7) and face-panel count (4-7) follow directly from the 100 mm lift thickness and 0.70 m face height; reinforcement strip count (6 interfaces) is at the designer's discretion within the competition envelope.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
